metadata.proposal_title: Single-Cell Informed Pathology Image Interpretation and Report Generation via Large-Scale Multimodal Foundation Models

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
PyTorch is aligned with the approved research topic, "large scale data analysis", and "Computational Science and Visualization" in general. 
This will be provided as part of PyTorch container image 25.11 from NGC which has pytroch 2.10 among other things.

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes: OpenSlide is a C library that provides a simple interface to read whole-slide images (also known as virtual slides)

checks.name: Prohibited Use Screening (LC 2.7)
checks.notes: No prohibited use indicated in the documentation.

checks.name: Restricted Entities Screening (LC 2.5)
checks.notes:
According to Github: https://github.com/openslide/openslide
Acknowledgements
OpenSlide has been developed by Carnegie Mellon University and other contributors.
OpenSlide has been supported by the National Institutes of Health and the Clinical and Translational Science Institute at the University of Pittsburgh.

It is not being maintained by multiple contributors on GitHub.

checks.name: Source / Provenance
checks.notes:
https://github.com/openslide/openslide
https://openslide.org/
well maintained, 2700+ commits, last PR accepted on October 28th, 2025,

checks.name: License / Permissions
checks.notes: GPL2 license. Fit for project use.

checks.name: Bundled Tools / Dependencies
checks.notes:
Dependencies for building OpenSlide:
GCC or Clang, Meson, cairo ≥ 1.2, glib ≥ 2.56, libdicom ≥ 1.2 (automatically built if missing), libjpeg-turbo ≥ 1.3 or libjpeg ≥ 9c, libpng, libtiff ≥ 4.0, libxml2, OpenJPEG ≥ 2.1, SQLite ≥ 3.14, zlib, Zstandard (by facebook)

most if not all of the above are provided by Shaheen III GPUP opreating system. The rest will by source built from sources downloaded from corresponding project's websites. 

It highly probably will be provided as a container image by KSL team.

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes: A CLI based runtime for interacting with a catalog of supported LLMs. For the project, it does not look mandatory, but is a more of a convenience  tool. Otherwise, the user will need to load the model in pytorch and run forward pass with inputs provided , for the case of inference, i.e. prompting.

checks.name: Prohibited Use Screening (LC 2.7)
checks.notes: The use of Ollama is not confined to one model but a catalog of supported models. Having said this, the project members will not have access to anyother model but the ones requested in the project proposal. Therefore, ollama's use is limited in scope.

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
SpeciesLLM is a transformer-based single-cell foundation model trained on large-scale transcriptomic data to learn gene and cell embeddings for biological analysis tasks.
The model supports the project objective of building a single-cell foundation model that aligns cellular transcriptomic embeddings with pathology image representations in a unified multimodal latent space.

checks.name: Prohibited Use Screening (LC 2.7)
checks.notes: No mention of sensitive terms in the documentation classified in Prohibited Use Screening in accordance with LC 2.7.

checks.name: Source / Provenance & D5+M Affiliation Screening (LC 2.5)
checks.notes: The model is developed and maintained by Gao Lab, an in-house research group led by Prof. Xin Gao at King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST).

checks.name: License / Permissions
checks.notes: Public open-source licensing terms are not explicitly specified in the provided documentation.

checks.name: FLOPS Calculation
checks.notes:
Number of trainable parameters: 500 x 10^6
Number of tokens: 9.83 x 10^10 

Estimated FLOPs= 6 x 500 x 10^6 x 9.83 x 10^10  = 2.9490 x 10^20

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
scGPT is an official generative pre-trained transformer for large-scale single-cell multi-omics that learns cell and gene representations and supports downstream tasks such as annotation, integration, and perturbation prediction.
It supports Objective 1 as one of the single-cell foundation models used to provide cell-state embeddings for cross-modal alignment with pathology images.

checks.name: Prohibited Use Screening (LC 2.7)
checks.notes: No mention of sensitive terms in the documentation classified in Prohibited Use Screening in accordance with LC 2.7.

checks.name: Source / Provenance & D5+M Affiliation Screening (LC 2.5)
checks.notes: scGPT is maintained by Bo Wang Lab at the University of Toronto, LinkedIn profile for Bo Wang: https://www.linkedin.com/in/bo-wang-a6065240/

checks.name: License / Permissions
checks.notes:
MIT license.
Commercial and research use are generally allowed.
Modification and redistribution are allowed with preservation of the license notice.

checks.name: Training Data Documentation
checks.notes: CELLxGENE release 2023-04-15 with 33M cells, by Chan-Zuckerberg Initiative.

checks.name: FLOPS Calculation
checks.notes:
Number of trainable parameters: 53 M  
Number of tokens: 9.12 x 10^10 

Estimated FLOPs= 6 x 53 x 10^6 x 9.12 x 10^10= 2.90016 x 10^19

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
Geneformer is a transformer foundation model pretrained on large-scale human single-cell transcriptomes to make context-aware predictions and reusable cell embeddings for network biology tasks.
It supports Objective 1 by serving as a single-cell embedding model for the unified multimodal latent space.

checks.name: Prohibited Use Screening (LC 2.7)
checks.notes: No mention of sensitive terms in the documentation classified in Prohibited Use Screening in accordance with LC 2.7.

checks.notes:

checks.name: Training Data Documentation
checks.notes: Genecorpus-30M for V1 and ~104M non-cancer human single-cell transcriptomes for V2, by Theodoris Lab.

checks.name: FLOPS Calculation
checks.notes:
Number of trainable parameters: 316x10^6
Number of tokens: 4.67x10^11 

Estimated FLOPs= 6 x 316x10^6 x 4.67x10^11 = 8.854 x 10^24

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
H-optimus-0 is an open-source histology foundation model from Bioptimus, built as a 1.1B-parameter vision transformer for extracting features from H&E whole-slide histology images for downstream pathology tasks.
It supports Objective 1 on the pathology side by providing WSI image embeddings to align with single-cell and spatial transcriptomic representations.

Owner: Bioptimus, a biotech AI company.
You need to agree to share your contact information to access this model.

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
HE2RNA is a multiple-instance learning model from Owkin that predicts transcriptomic profiles from H&E pathology slides.
It is best treated as a baseline/comparison model for Objective 1, because the proposal explicitly discusses HE2RNA as prior histology-to-expression work that motivates the proposed multimodal alignment approach.

This repository was archived by the owner on Feb 15, 2023. It is now read-only.

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
Llama 3.1 is Meta’s general-purpose open-weight LLM family for commercial and research use, intended for assistant-like chat and broader natural-language generation tasks.
It aligns with Objective 2, where the proposal calls for a medical-report LLM fine-tuned to generate structured pathology reports and biologically grounded explanations.

checks.name: Prohibited Use Screening (LC 2.7)
checks.notes: Sensitive-use restrictions are explicitly present in Meta’s documentation, including prohibited uses involving military, warfare, nuclear applications, espionage, and guns / illegal weapons.

checks.name: Source / Provenance & D5+M Affiliation Screening (LC 2.5)
checks.notes:
https://huggingface.co/meta-llama (The link provided is for the entire Llama family page)
Maintained by: Meta Llama non-D5 country.
Gated Access, requires permission.

checks.name: License / Permissions
checks.notes:
Licensed under the Llama 3.1 Community License.
Commercial and research use are allowed subject to the license and Acceptable Use Policy.
Fine-tuning and synthetic-data / distillation use are allowed.
Use must comply with the Acceptable Use Policy, including the prohibited-use categories above.
